title: chrysothamnus nauseosus subsp. bigelovii
intro: Chrysothamnus nauseosus subsp. bigelovii, also known as Bigelow's rubber rabbitbrush, is a shrub plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of colds, fever, and digestive issues, due to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, protect against oxidative stress, and prevent the growth of microorganisms. Herbal preparations of Bigelow's rubber rabbitbrush can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal effects.
